extraction.one_line_summary: MOHORIČ won solo after early neutralizations from crashes, his second individual road stage win of this Tour
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR enforced neutralization after KWIATKOWSKI attacked during crash delays, demonstrating yellow jersey patron power dynamics
MOHORIČ winning two solo road stages same Tour reveals tactical gap exploitation skills rare outside pure sprinter/climber categories
Multiple mass crashes on flat stages forced Alpecin-Fenix to repeatedly ease pace, exposing sprinter team control vulnerabilities

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Controlled race tempo and enforced neutralizations after crashes, projecting authority with 5:45 lead before final TT
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Second place finish means record-breaking 35th win depends entirely on Paris sprint after four stage wins already
rider_signals.MOHORIČ Matej: positive: Second solo breakaway win demonstrates exceptional ability to finish from distance, rare tactical versatility
rider_signals.VAN MOER Brent: negative: Caused massive early crash by failing to brake, creating tactical chaos that required yellow jersey intervention
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: negative: Attacked while THOMAS and teammates delayed in crash, forced to stop by POGAČAR's patron intervention
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: neutral: Caught in second crash while contesting green jersey points with only Paris stage remaining
